title: WKWebView Stage 6: finalize + delete legacy code
description: Confirm zoom/print. Delete DOMNode+Text.{h,m}, WebView+WebViewPrivateHeaders.h, MPMathJaxListener.{h,m}, the MathJax redirect, the WebView (Shortcut) category, legacy Web*Delegate conformances, kMPMathJaxCDN, unused JavaScriptCore import. Drop deleted files from the Xcode target. Clean build, zero legacy WebView references.
close_reason: Deleted dead legacy-WebView code: DOMNode+Text.{h,m}, WebView+WebViewPrivateHeaders.h, MPMathJaxListener.{h,m} (all zero call sites), their 14 pbxproj entries, and the #if 0 DOMDocument/DOMNode block in MPDocument.m. Clean build, 53/53 tests pass, zero compiled legacy WebView references. DEVIATIONS from bead text (verified against code): kMPMathJaxCDN is KEPT (still loads MathJax from CDN in mathjaxScripts); the JavaScriptCore import is KEPT (active JSContext use in MPUtilities.m) -- neither is 'unused'; the 'WebView (Shortcut) category' and 'Web*Delegate conformances' don't exist (already removed in earlier stages); the MathJax redirect was already gone. Zoom/print unaffected -- both run through self.preview (WKWebView) APIs untouched here.

title: WKWebView Stage 5: scroll-sync rebuild (async metrics)
description: Editor side of updateHeaderLocations stays sync; preview header tops + cached scrollHeight/innerHeight fetched async via injected JS. Drive preview via fire-and-forget window.scrollTo. Warm cache on willStartLiveScroll:.
notes:
From PR #5 review (CodeRabbit/Codex): make MathJax completion load-exact. init.js posts a constant sentinel (postMessage(1)), so a stale mathJaxEnd from a superseded load can satisfy the next load's completion guard early. Fix as part of the scroll-sync rebuild: inject a per-load token (e.g. a per-load WKUserScript at documentStart setting window.__mpLoadToken=N, or evaluateJavaScript), have init.js post that token, and validate it in -userContentController:didReceiveScriptMessage: against the current generation before latching completion. Currently inert (scroll-sync stubbed; every load's completion incl. the 3s fallback re-enables window flush, so no permanent freeze).
Update (PR #5 round 2): added a previewNavigationFinished gate on the mathJaxEnd handler — a stale message arriving during the next load's provisional phase (navFinished=NO) is now rejected, which eliminates the freeze risk. Residual: a stale message arriving in the narrow window between the new load's didFinish and its own mathJaxEnd can still complete it slightly early (inert while scroll-sync stubbed). The full per-load token (host-injected, validated in the handler) closes that last window if the scroll-sync rebuild needs exact timing.
RESOLVED in stage 3 (PR #5 round 4): the per-load token is now implemented. didProduceHTMLOutput bumps previewLoadGeneration and injects window.__mpLoadToken via a documentStart WKUserScript; init.js echoes it on mathJaxEnd; the handler rejects messages whose token != current generation. So scroll-sync (.5.4) no longer needs to build per-load completion identity — it can rely on the existing exactly-once completion firing after MathJax for the correct load. Token round-trip runtime-verified (token==gen on every message).
close_reason: Scroll-sync rebuilt for WKWebView (async metrics + window.scrollTo); runtime-verified bidirectional tracking

title: WKWebView Stage 4: async word count + divider background color
description: Port DOMNode+Text tree walk to injected JS (Intl.Segmenter; preserve PRE>CODE=0 and inline-code=1 rules; diff against old counts). updateWordCount async via evaluateJavaScript:completionHandler:. Cache body background color, refresh on load; redrawDivider reads cache.
close_reason: Ported DOMNode+Text walk to injected JS (Intl.Segmenter, PRE>CODE=0/inline-code=1 preserved, runtime-verified words=4/chars=32/noSpace=29); updateWordCount async via evaluateJavaScript; cached body background color refreshed on load, redrawDivider reads cache. Local code-review fixes (weak-self) applied. 45/45 tests pass.

title: WKWebView Stage 3: MathJax completion via WKScriptMessageHandler
description: Replace windowScriptObject MathJaxListener injection with WKScriptMessageHandler + WKUserScript at config time. init.js posts window.webkit.messageHandlers.mathJaxEnd.postMessage(1). Completion handler runs exactly once per load (per-load token) whether MathJax is on or off.
close_reason: MathJax typeset-complete wired through WKScriptMessageHandler (weak wrapper, exactly-once via per-load generation/flag, reset at didStartProvisional, -close teardown). init.js posts mathJaxEnd. Stock CDN MathJax 2.7.3 fires 'End', so the #548 bundled-patch workaround is unneeded. Runtime-verified exactly-once; 45 tests pass.

title: WKWebView Stage 1: scaffolding + asset loading + navigation
description: Replace legacy WebView with a programmatically-created WKWebView. New MPAssetSchemeHandler (WKURLSchemeHandler) serves app-bundle + Application Support assets over macdown-asset: with a path-security gate. MPAsset/MPRenderer emit macdown-asset: URLs for FullLink bundle/AppSupport assets (Embedded export path untouched); MathJax preview served from bundle via the scheme (drops CDN+redirect for preview). Load via loadHTMLString:baseURL: with doc-dir baseURL for relative images (verify on 12.0; fallback macdown-doc: scheme). WKNavigationDelegate replaces WebFrameLoadDelegate+WebPolicyDelegate. Keep zoom (pageZoom) and print (printOperationWithPrintInfo:) working. Temporarily stub word count, scroll-sync, divider color, copy-as-HTML (each tracked by a later-stage bead).
notes: Correction: MathJax preview is NOT served from the bundle in Stage 1. The bundle ships only a patched MathJax.js (for issue #548) that the legacy WebResourceLoadDelegate swapped in; WKWebView has no equivalent, so MathJax loads from the CDN as before. Reinstating the patch / offline bundling is macdown-8tk.5.2.
close_reason: WKWebView scaffolding + macdown-asset scheme handler + navigation landed and verified (CSS, Prism, mermaid, MathJax, relative images, tables render; editorOnRight + link-cancel review fixes confirmed). Later stages tracked by sibling beads; drop-prevention .5.6 and off-main-thread reads .5.7 filed from review.

title: Migrate preview from legacy WebView to WKWebView
description: The big architectural migration. ~60 deprecation warnings in Document/MPDocument.m and Extension/DOMNode+Text.m (WebView, WebFrame, Web*Delegate, DOMDocument/DOMNode). WKWebView's async, process-isolated model changes how the preview receives HTML and how the editor reads the rendered DOM. Subsumes all WebView/DOM/WebFrame deprecation cleanup (so issue A excludes those). Will likely be broken into sub-issues.
close_reason: WKWebView migration complete: all stages (1-6) and child bugs closed. Preview runs entirely on WKWebView, legacy WebView/DOM code deleted (8tk.5.5), scroll-sync rebuilt and the reference-node asymmetry fixed (4y8, PR #12, runtime-verified). Remaining scroll-sync edge cases tracked in macdown-y9j.

title: Clean up mechanical AppKit deprecation warnings
description: Replace deprecated AppKit APIs with documented modern equivalents: NSOnState, NSDragPboard/NSFilenamesPboardType, allowedFileTypes->allowedContentTypes, NSFileHandlingPanelOKButton, insertText:->insertText:replacementRange:, unarchiveObjectWithFile:->unarchivedObjectOfClass:fromData:error:, colorUsingColorSpaceName:->colorUsingColorSpace:, base64Encoding->base64EncodedStringWithOptions:, NSToolbarItem maxSize, NSAutoPagination, isFlushWindowDisabled/enableFlushWindow. EXCLUDES WebView/DOM/WebFrame deprecations -- those are resolved by the WKWebView migration, not here. Good candidate for the next PR.
close_reason: Mechanical AppKit deprecations replaced with modern APIs; WebView/DOM + flush-window deferred to macdown-8tk.5, C prototypes to macdown-8tk.2, drag-and-drop fixes to macdown-8tk.10.

title: Revive MacDown on a modern toolchain (Xcode 27+)
description: Umbrella for bringing the ~5-year-dormant codebase up to date. PR #1 (merged) got it building on Xcode 27 with a macOS 12 floor. Remaining modernization work is tracked as children. Prose roadmap lives in CLAUDE.md 'Revival notes'.

title: Characterization tests for NSTextView+Autocomplete matching/wrap/markup/header logic
description: Round out the editor characterization safety net started by macdown-6st (list/indent) to cover the rest of NSTextView+Autocomplete.m before the Swift-interop pass touches it: auto-pairing of brackets/quotes (completeMatchingCharacterForText: incl. the smart-quote-substitution branch), selection wrapping (wrapMatchingCharactersOfCharacter:/wrapTextInRange:, markup chars + strikethrough), deleteMatchingCharactersAround:, the emphasis/strong markup toggle (toggleForMarkupPrefix: + substringInRange:isSurroundedByPrefix:suffix: with its */**/*** special-casing), toggleBlockWithPattern:, makeHeaderForSelectedLinesWithLevel:, and the insertMappedContent guard paths. Folded into MPAutocompleteTests.m in the same PR as 6st.
close_reason: Extended MPAutocompleteTests.m with 23 tests: auto-pairing (incl. smart-quote branch), insert/wrap dispatcher, selection wrapping + strikethrough, deleteMatchingCharactersAround, substringInRange:isSurroundedBy emphasis/strong special-casing, markup + block toggles, makeHeader (add/replace/strip), insertMappedContent guard paths.

title: Modernization direction: SPM packages + thin AppKit shell
description:
DIRECTION (not a fixed destination), set 2026-06-14. Move MacDown from a monolithic Obj-C + CocoaPods Xcode target toward most code living in local SPM packages (Obj-C and/or Swift, even SwiftUI), with the Xcode app target reduced to a thin shell: app lifecycle, Info.plist, entitlements, code signing, the main menu / nibs, and linking the packages. This is a direction to steer by, taken opportunistically; it is explicitly NOT a commitment to a pure-Swift or pure-SwiftUI rewrite.

THREE INDEPENDENT AXES (do not conflate):
1. Language: Obj-C -> Swift, chosen per class, never forced.
2. Packaging: one app target -> SPM packages + thin shell.
3. Dependencies: CocoaPods -> SPM.

THE PHYSICS (constraints that dictate ordering):
- An SPM target is single-language (Obj-C/C OR Swift). Mixed language = separate targets in the same package with a dependency edge. A class's language is just "which target it lives in."
- Swift-depends-on-Obj-C is clean (SPM generates the modulemap). Obj-C-depends-up-on-Swift across a module boundary has no clean story. So migrate leaves-up and keep new Swift ABOVE old Obj-C; when an up-call is unavoidable, do it inside the app target (bridging header / @objc), not across a package.
- An SPM package cannot depend on a CocoaPod. So a class cannot move into a package until its pod dependencies are already on SPM. This couples the de-pod work (axis 3) to modularization (axis 2): de-podding the leaves is the prerequisite that unblocks moving the MP* code.

PHASES (each opportunistic; do when there is a reason):
- Phase 0 - Enable Swift/Obj-C interop (bridging header on first .swift + incremental nullability on imported headers). See bead for this. ~30 min, gated on first real Swift code.
- Phase 1 - Stand up local SPM package(s) and link from the app target.
- Phase 2 - De-pod the leaves to SPM (Sparkle binary; hoedown/LibYAML vendored as SPM clang targets; handlebars-objc/PAPreferences/MASPreferences vendored or replaced). This is what unblocks Phase 3.
- Phase 3 - Move modules into packages AS-IS (Obj-C stays Obj-C). Moving a class and converting its language become two separate decisions.
- Phase 4 - Convert opportunistically behind stable package APIs; new/rewritten UI as SwiftUI hosted in NSHostingController (e.g. PDF export, preferences).
- Phase 5 - Retire CocoaPods / the .xcworkspace once the last pod is on SPM; the Xcode target becomes the thin shell.

REALISTIC END STATE = HYBRID (correct, not a compromise): NSDocument/MPDocument, the NSTextView editor, and the WKWebView preview stay AppKit (likely Obj-C) for a long time; new leaf features and UI go Swift/SwiftUI. "Thin Xcode wrapper" holds for most code, but the document/editor core remains a substantial AppKit citizen of the app target.

HOW EXISTING BEADS MAP:
- Sparkle -> SPM is the first concrete de-pod: see macdown-8tk.4 (restore Check-for-Updates) and macdown-8tk.8 (fork release identity / EdDSA keys + own SUFeedURL). Decision: go Sparkle 2 (2.9.3) via SPM, NOT a 1.x bump.
- macdown-8tk.7 reframed from "bump pods" to "de-pod to SPM (audit each)".
- macdown-ppi.1 (PDF export) is the likely first Swift/SwiftUI component (Phase 4); depends on Phase 0 interop.
- macdown-8tk.9 (Modern Obj-C Converter) is ORTHOGONAL and optional - cosmetic syntax only, never a gate for Swift.
- Characterization tests (macdown-6st, and the closed 8tk.11) are the safety net that makes module moves / rewrites safe.

title: MacDownTests bundle unsigned: xcodebuild test fails to load on Apple Silicon
description:
The MacDownTests target sets CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY = "" (pbxproj lines 1749 & 1767, Debug+Release), added by upstream commit 2265422 (Jan 2020, 'Prevent code-signing instead of remove it'). This emits an UNSIGNED MacDownTests.xctest. On Apple Silicon, dyld refuses to load an unsigned bundle into the ad-hoc-signed test host, so 'xcodebuild test' from the CLI fails: 'The bundle MacDownTests.xctest couldn't be loaded ... code signature ... not valid for use in process: Trying to load an unsigned library'.

Proven: re-running with CLI override CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY="-" makes all 53 tests pass. Predates all revival work; not caused by any recent PR. The Xcode IDE/MCP path apparently signs/injects differently, which is why interactive runs succeeded.

Fix: set the test target's CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY to "-" (ad-hoc / Sign to Run Locally) in both configs, removing the obsolete 2020 empty-identity workaround. Needed for CI (macdown-8tk.6) to run tests via xcodebuild.
close_reason: Added CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY = "-" (ad-hoc) to both MacDownTests configs. Clean 'xcodebuild test' now signs the bundle and passes all 53 tests with no CLI override.

title: Revival scrambled the Settings/Preferences window control layout
description:
The revival (Xcode 27 / macOS SDK 27 build) has scrambled the control layout in the Settings (Preferences) window — controls are misaligned/mispositioned across the preference panes. Likely fallout from modern AppKit/Interface Builder reflowing the old xibs (autoresizing vs Auto Layout, intrinsic sizes, or deployment-target rendering changes) under the new SDK.

Action: compare the original app's Preferences window (all panes: General, Editor, Markdown, Rendering/HTML, Terminal) against the current build, pane by pane, and restore the intended layout (or improve on it). The pref panes are MASPreferences-based view controllers with per-pane xibs in MacDown/Localization/Base.lproj/ (MP*PreferencesViewController.xib). Related: macdown-2a9 already cleaned up stale Terminal-prefs outlets in that area.

Acceptance: each preference pane's controls are correctly aligned and sized, matching (or bettering) the original app, with no clipping/overlap, at the default window size.
notes:
CORRECTED DIAGNOSIS (2026-06-14) — supersedes earlier note.

My first diagnosis (window collapses too narrow -> toolbar overflow + overlap) was WRONG. Proven by controlled A/B with lldb on the running Debug build:
- WITH width constraints:  prefs window 405x433, contentView 405x367, fittingSize.w=405.
- WITHOUT (stashed/rebuilt): prefs window 405x433, contentView 405x367, fittingSize.w=400.
The window is 405pt (the HTML/Rendering pane's design width) in BOTH cases. It was never collapsing. I was misled by (a) this Mac running a low-DPI ~2560pt-wide desktop, which makes a correct 405pt window LOOK tiny in screenshots, and (b) accidentally comparing against the original 2020 Release app in /Applications (com.uranusjr.macdown, build 1008.4) which I'd launched via open_application.

The width-constraint approach changes nothing; abandon it. (Currently sitting in a git stash on branch revival/fix-preferences-layout; recommend dropping.)

ACTUAL visible regressions (same XIBs, modern SDK vs the 2020 binary), to re-investigate:
1. NSToolbar overflows to a single item + '>>' chevron, although the 2020 binary shows all 5 pane items even on narrower panes (Terminal=360pt). Likely Big Sur+ toolbar item sizing/spacing or a missing NSWindowToolbarStyle.
2. 'Syntax highlighted code block' checkbox overlaps the CSS row in the Rendering pane (rest of the pane lays out fine). Likely Big Sur+ control-metric inflation (taller checkboxes/popups) against tight fixed gaps / a missing vertical constraint — consistent with the General pane's hardcoded height=14 checkbox constraints.

Net: this is control-metric/toolbar modernization (closer to an IB refresh), NOT a window-width fix. Needs fresh approach + verification ONLY against the Debug build (never the /Applications Release app).
close_reason: Fixed Settings/Preferences window layout under modern AppKit: toolbar overflow (NSWindowToolbarStylePreference), Rendering pane control overlap + Graphviz/Mermaid alignment/spacing, pane width, and renamed window to 'Settings'. Follow-up localization refresh tracked separately.

title: Broaden local-resource access under WKWebView (parent-relative + explicit file:// body URLs)
description: The WKWebView asset scheme gate serves only the document directory SUBTREE (plus bundle/App-Support/temp), which is stricter than the old WebView (no gate, any file readable). Two regressions follow: (1) parent/sibling-relative resources like ../images/photo.png resolve outside documentDirectory and are rejected (NSURLErrorResourceUnavailable); (2) explicit file:// URLs in the body (e.g. inserted by toggleImage: from a Finder URL on the pasteboard, or hand-written file:///...) stay file:// and WKWebView blocks them from the scheme-origin page, so only MPAsset-emitted links get rewritten. Fixing needs a deliberate security-vs-compatibility decision: how far outside the doc dir to allow (parent levels? whole volume? per-document grant/preference?) and whether to rewrite local body URLs (file://, bare-absolute) to macdown-asset before loading. Surfaced in PR #4 review (Codex P2 x2).
close_reason: Implemented 3-level local-file access scope (document subtree / +parent / any file) honored by the asset-scheme gate, plus file:// body-URL rewriting to macdown-asset. Shipped on revival/wkwebview-local-asset-scope.

title: Prevent file/content drops into the WKWebView preview
description: Regression from the WKWebView migration (Stage 1). The legacy WebUIDelegate dragDestinationActionMaskForDraggingInfo: returned WebDragDestinationActionNone to block drops INTO the preview. WKWebView has no equivalent and accepts drops by default; dragging a file/text from Finder onto the preview pane can navigate it away from the rendered document. Needs a drop-prevention approach (navigation guard for non-link WKNavigationTypeOther whose URL != current base, or refusing drag types on the web view). Deferred from Stage 1 review because verifying drag-drop behavior needs manual UI testing.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #4 review round 1: decidePolicyForNavigationAction cancels unexpected main-frame navigations (non-asset-scheme, non about:blank), blocking file/text drops into the preview.

title: Characterization tests for NSTextView+Autocomplete list/indent logic
description: Split from macdown-8tk.11 (target #2). Add characterization tests pinning the CURRENT behaviour of the list-continuation and indentation logic in NSTextView+Autocomplete.m -- pure text manipulation, currently untested, modified during the PR2 deprecation cleanup. Goal: lock the editor's auto-list/indent behaviour so future edits (and the Swift-interop pass) don't silently change it.
notes: macdown-8tk.11 delivered the MPRenderer markdown->HTML characterization suite (MPRendererTests.m, 17 tests). This bead carries the remaining editor-side target.
close_reason: Added MPAutocompleteTests.m: 21 characterization tests pinning current behaviour of list/blockquote/indent continuation (completeNextListItem/BlockquoteLine/IndentedLine) and indentation (insertSpacesForTab, unindentForSpacesBefore, indent/unindentSelectedLines).

title: Expand automated tests (characterization safety net for modernization)
description:
Current coverage (20 tests) is narrow utility code (MPAsset, MPColor, MPHTMLTabularize, MPPreferences font, MPStringLookup, one MPUtility JS helper). The modernization touches untested core code.

Goal: add CHARACTERIZATION tests that pin CURRENT behaviour so modernization doesn't silently change it (not new-feature TDD).

Priority targets:
1. MPRenderer markdown -> HTML. This is the key seam: MPRenderer produces HTML as a plain string, independent of the WebView, so 'markdown X -> HTML contains Y' tests pin rendering behaviour. Doing this BEFORE the WKWebView migration (macdown-8tk.5) lets us prove the HTML generation is unchanged and only the display layer changed -- turning the riskiest migration from a visual guess into a passing baseline.
2. NSTextView+Autocomplete list-continuation/indent logic (pure text manipulation, currently untested, just modified in the deprecation-cleanup PR).

Compounds with CI (macdown-8tk.6): tests only auto-prevent regressions once CI runs them on every PR. Recommend doing the MPRenderer tests before macdown-8tk.5.
close_reason: Added MPRenderer markdown->HTML characterization suite (MPRendererTests.m, 19 tests; full suite 39 passing). Editor-side autocomplete tests split to macdown-6st.

title: Restore Check-for-Updates (SUUpdater not loading from nib)
description: Launch log: 'Unknown class SUUpdater ... MainMenu.nib' and 'does not respond to checkForUpdates:'. Sparkle's SUUpdater is not registered, so Check-for-Updates is dead. Likely needs Sparkle wiring updated; relates to bumping the Sparkle pod.
notes:
ROOT CAUSE DIAGNOSED (2026-06-14), deferred to fold into macdown-8tk.8 (fork release identity). Two compounding faults:
1. ARCH: the pinned Sparkle 1.18.1 framework binary is NON-FAT x86_64-only (lipo confirms) — no arm64 slice. On Apple Silicon (arm64 build) it cannot be linked or loaded at all, so SUUpdater's class is never registered. The linker was silently warning+ignoring the wrong-arch framework ('ld: warning: ignoring file Sparkle.framework/Sparkle: found architecture x86_64, required arm64').
2. DEAD-STRIP: even with a universal Sparkle, SUUpdater is referenced ONLY by MainMenu.xib (runtime class lookup) and a delegate-method pointer type — never by a compiled symbol — so the modern linker's -dead_strip_dylibs drops Sparkle's load command (otool -L shows no Sparkle LC_LOAD_DYLIB).

NIB WIRING IS INTACT/CORRECT: MainMenu.xib has an SUUpdater customObject (Zfp-OK-9bN) whose delegate outlet -> MPMainController (eq0-c4-vgQ); the Check-for-Updates menu item sends checkForUpdates: to it. MPMainController already implements feedURLStringForUpdater:. So no nib/code wiring changes needed for Sparkle 1.x.

FIX (when doing 8tk.8): (a) bump Sparkle pod to a UNIVERSAL build. Sparkle pod is from CocoaPods TRUNK (free to bump). Options: 1.27.3 = last universal 1.x, keeps SUUpdater (drop-in, minimal); or 2.9.3 = modern/maintained but requires migrating nib SUUpdater->SPUStandardUpdaterController and MPMainController SUUpdaterDelegate->SPUUpdaterDelegate. (b) Add a force-link reference so dead-strip can't drop it: '(void)[SUUpdater class];' in MPMainController applicationDidFinishLaunching (timing irrelevant; the link-time symbol ref forces dyld to load Sparkle at launch), or '-Wl,-needed_framework,Sparkle' in the target OTHER_LDFLAGS. (c) Updates won't actually deliver until the fork has its own SUFeedURL + signing keys (the whole reason this folds into 8tk.8; current feed = macdown.uranusjr.com, original author's; dsa_pub.pem is the original's — Sparkle 2.x uses EdDSA instead).
A draft of fix (b) was prototyped on branch revival/sparkle-check-for-updates (deleted) — it built-failed as EXPECTED, proving fault #1 (undefined _OBJC_CLASS_$_SUUpdater for arm64).

title: Investigate imagePNG_error_break errors at launch
description: App logs '[MacDown] imagePNG_error_break' 3x on launch (libpng error breakpoint). Find the source -- a bundled PNG modern libpng rejects, or an asset load path -- then decide fix vs. benign.
notes:
INVESTIGATION (2026-06-14): Could not reproduce. Findings:
- imagePNG_error_break is an ImageIO (CoreGraphics/libpng) symbol, NOT MacDown's. Non-fatal; appears only in the debugger console (never in unified logging or process stderr), so invisible to end users.
- All 78 bundled PNGs pass strict pngcheck AND ImageIO re-encode (sips) with zero warnings. No loose PNGs in the built .app (icons compile into Assets.car). So no corrupt MacDown asset exists.
- Did NOT fire on a bare launch under lldb (symbolic breakpoint on imagePNG_error_break, x2 runs), nor in a full Xcode run by the owner (console showed only benign sandbox/XPC/WKWebView noise + the SUUpdater nib errors; multiple WebContent processes spun up = previews loaded; still no imagePNG hit).
- Most likely the original '3x on launch' load path lived in the legacy WebView code and was removed during the WKWebView migration.
RECOMMENDATION: close as not-reproducible / no malformed asset. If it ever recurs, the one-step diagnosis is a symbolic breakpoint on imagePNG_error_break with action 'bt 20' + auto-continue (Xcode) — the backtrace names the loader.
SIDE FINDING: same Xcode console shows MPTerminalPreferencesViewController has two dead outlets (location, supportText -> 'missing setter or instance variable') — unrelated minor nib issue, candidate for its own bead.
close_reason: Not reproducible under lldb or Xcode; all 78 bundled PNGs verified clean (pngcheck + sips); no loose PNGs in bundle. ImageIO debugger-only diagnostic, invisible to users, likely a legacy-WebView image load removed by the WKWebView migration. Recurrence recipe documented in notes.

title: Fix C function-declaration-without-prototype warnings
description: Add (void) to empty C parameter lists. Sites: Utility/MPUtilities.m, Document/MPRenderer.m, Application/MPMainController.m, Preferences/MPHtmlPreferencesViewController.m, Document/MPDocument.m, Extension/hoedown_html_patch.c, and vendored Dependency/peg-markdown-highlight/pmh_*.c (decide: patch vendored sources or suppress for those).
close_reason: Added (void) to 13 empty C parameter lists across MacDown sources and vendored pmh parser (source pmh_parser_head.c, not the generated pmh_parser.c). Build clean, zero strict-prototype warnings.

title: Vendor GBCli as a local SPM package (de-pod)
description: Per-pod step of macdown-8tk.7. GBCli (1.1, MIT) is a small Obj-C command-line parsing library used only by the macdown-cmd target (main.m, MPArgumentProcessor.m). Vendor the source as a local SPM package (Packages/GBCli) following the Hoedown/JJPluralForm de-pod template; drop the pod from the macdown-cmd target in the Podfile. Fully isolated from the app target.
close_reason: Vendored GBCli 1.1 as Packages/GBCli (local SPM); macdown-cmd fully de-integrated from CocoaPods; macdown-cmd + MacDown build, 118 tests pass.

title: Vendor JJPluralForm as a local SPM package (de-pod)
description: Per-pod step of macdown-8tk.7. JJPluralForm (2.1, MPL-2.0) is a 2-file pure-ObjC pluralization utility with a single consumer (MPDocument.m word-count). Vendor the source as a local SPM package (Packages/JJPluralForm) following the Hoedown template, drop the pod from the Podfile. Vendor rather than replace: it is wired into the Transifex localization system via JJ_PLURAL_FORM_RULE and ;-separated plural-form strings in every .lproj, which we must not hand-edit.
close_reason: Vendored JJPluralForm 2.1 as Packages/JJPluralForm (local SPM); dropped pod; build + 118 tests pass.

title: Guard front-matter consumers against non-dictionary YAML (objectForKey: crash)
description: Pre-existing latent crash surfaced during macdown-5mi review (not a regression). When a document's front matter is a top-level YAML SCALAR or SEQUENCE (e.g. '---\nfoo\n---'), -[NSString frontMatter:] returns an NSString/NSArray. MPDocument.m:~2260 then calls [[... objectForKey:@"title"] description] and MPRenderer.m calls [frontMatter HTMLTable]; objectForKey: on an NSString is an unrecognized selector -> crash (e.g. computing presumedFileName for an unsaved doc with Detect Front Matter on). Existed identically with the old libyaml/YAMLSerialization path. Fix: guard the title lookup (and any dictionary assumption) with isKindOfClass:[NSDictionary class] (M13OrderedDictionary is an NSDictionary subclass) before objectForKey:. Low priority; pathological input.

title: Replace LibYAML with swift-yaml (pure-Swift front-matter parsing)
description: Follow-up to macdown-5xp.3 (hoedown), which established the C-dependency de-pod template. Apply the same pattern to LibYAML (currently 'pod LibYAML, ~> 0.1' from the patched MacDownApp/cocoapods-specs repo, consumed via the app's vendored YAMLSerialization.m). Vendor the LibYAML C source into a local SPM clang target we own, expose its headers so existing imports resolve, remove the pod, and rebuild + test. Part of the de-pod axis of epic macdown-5xp / macdown-8tk.7.
design:
REPLACEMENT vs vendor (analysis 2026-06-15). Given the tiny use surface (NSString -frontMatter: -> ordered dict; render as HTML table; extract 'title'), replacing libyaml is viable. Options:

1. Yams (jpsim) — 1251 stars, maintained (2026-05), MIT, used by SwiftPM/SwiftLint. BUT it is libyaml-backed (bundles a CYaml target), so it does NOT remove pointer C; it swaps our frozen 0.1.4 for a maintained, fuzzed libyaml behind a Swift API. Lowest-risk modernization.
2. swift-yaml (1amageek) — TRUE pure Swift, YAML 1.2.2, zero deps, 569 tests, MIT, v1.0.1 (2026-04). Eliminates all C. RISK: only 1 star / unproven; requires swift-tools 6.2 (OK on CI Xcode 26.5).
3. Hand-rolled mini-parser — not recommended (YAML edge cases: anchors, flow collections, quoting).

HIDDEN REQUIREMENT: front matter is rendered as an HTML table that must PRESERVE KEY ORDER (current code uses M13OrderedDictionary for this). Any replacement must use an order-preserving Node API, NOT Codable-to-[String:Any] (unordered). Yams Node mapping preserves order; verify swift-yaml does too.

CLEANUP: replacing drops the vendored Dependency/YAML-framework/YAMLSerialization.{h,m} Obj-C wrapper + frozen libyaml. Does NOT drop M13OrderedDictionary (also used by NSObject+HTMLTabularize.m). Yams keeps C (CYaml); swift-yaml removes C entirely.

NEXT: data-driven spike — diff swift-yaml vs libyaml/Yams output on representative front matter (nested maps, tags:[a,b], quoted/multiline scalars, ... terminator) + existing fixtures, before choosing.
notes:
SPIKE RESULTS (2026-06-15, data-driven): Built a throwaway SPM package parsing 73 front-matter samples through BOTH Yams 5.4 (libyaml-backed = ground truth for current behavior) and swift-yaml 1.0.1 (pure Swift), comparing ordered keys + 'title' scalar + full normalized tree (MacDown's exact use).

Corpus: 53 REAL Jekyll front-matter blocks from /Volumes/Campfire/AntigenPlus/ap-docs (layout/title/nav_order/parent/last_modified_date timestamps/bools) + 12 synthetic shapes (flow & block sequences, nested maps, quoted colons, single-quote escapes, literal | and folded > scalars, unicode, empty value, bool/num, date, '100%') + 8 adversarial YAML footguns (Norway problem no/yes/on/off, sexagesimal time 12:30:00, leading-zero/hex/octal, .inf/.nan, nulls/~, duplicate keys, escapes, 1_000).

RESULT: 72/73 fully identical. 0 title mismatches. 0 key-order mismatches on all valid docs. swift-yaml threw 0; Yams threw 1.
The ONE divergence = duplicate keys (a:1 / a:3): Yams/libyaml THROWS (so current MacDown silently drops ALL front matter + title for such a doc, since objectsWithYAMLString:error:NULL returns nil), while swift-yaml is lenient and parses it. swift-yaml is thus MORE robust here; harmless-to-better for MacDown.
The classic 1.1-vs-1.2 footguns (Norway, sexagesimal) did NOT diverge because MacDown only ever reads the raw scalar STRING (kYAMLReadOptionStringScalars + stringify), which both libs preserve identically.

TOOLCHAIN: swift-yaml compiled clean on Swift 6.4; declares NO platform floor, so macOS 12 is fine. Requires swift-tools 6.2 (OK: CI Xcode 26.5).

UPDATED RECOMMENDATION: data supports swift-yaml as a viable pure-Swift drop-in for this narrow use (eliminates ALL C + the Obj-C YAMLSerialization wrapper). Residual concern is bus-factor (1 GitHub star), MITIGATED by: MIT + pure Swift + zero deps + tiny integration surface => trivially vendorable/forkable if abandoned, unlike a C lib. Fallback if risk-averse: Yams (maintained, but keeps libyaml/C). Keep characterization tests (MPRendererTests front-matter + title) as the safety net for whichever is chosen.
close_reason: Replaced LibYAML with pure-Swift swift-yaml; MPFrontMatterParser bridges to -[NSString frontMatter:]. All C removed from front-matter parsing. Build + 117 tests green; data-driven spike validated parity.

title: Print settings pane strings missing from Localizable.strings / Transifex
description: The programmatically-built Print settings pane (MPPrintPreferencesViewController, from macdown-ppi.1) uses NSLocalizedString throughout — section header, footnote, 'Top:/Left:/Bottom:/Right:' labels, toolbar 'Print' — but NONE of these keys exist in MacDown/Localization/*.lproj/Localizable.strings. They render via the English base-language fallback (key == English text), so English is correct but no translation is possible. macdown-wh6 added one more such key ('Default print & PDF margins (%@)'), consistent with the existing gap. Fix: run genstrings over the pane, land the keys in en.lproj/Localizable.strings, and push to Transifex so translators can pick them up. Out of scope for wh6 (this pre-dates it across the whole pane).

title: Locale-aware margin units (metric) in the Print settings pane
description:
The Print settings pane (MPPrintPreferencesViewController) hard-codes inches:
the header says "(inches)", the field suffix is "in", and the points<->display
transformer divides by 72. Margins are stored in points (correct), but metric
users (most locales) must convert mm to inches by hand.

Make the displayed unit locale-aware: derive inches vs millimetres/centimetres
from NSLocale / NSMeasurementFormatter (Swift Measurement<UnitLength> is a
natural fit, since this code is already crossing into Swift), updating the
header text, the field suffix label, and the conversion factor accordingly.
Surfaced by code review of the ppi.1 print work; an i18n enhancement, out of
scope for the initial inch-based pane.
close_reason: Locale-aware margin unit (inches/centimetres) via MPMarginUnit Swift helper; pane derives header, suffix, and conversion factor from it.

title: Re-clamp print margins when the print panel changes paper/orientation
description:
Print margins are clamped to the printer's imageable area in MPDocument
-printInfo, which runs against the document's *default* paper. The standard
print panel lets the user change paper size and orientation; those choices are
merged into the print info AFTER the clamp (in -printOperationWithSettings:),
so the margins are not re-clamped for the newly chosen paper. On a smaller
sheet the 1" margins can again exceed the imageable area (the clipping ppi.1
fixed), and on a larger/landscape sheet they may be over-margined.

Proper fix needs a print-panel accessory / NSPrintPanel delegate (or
-preparePageLayout:) that re-runs MPPrintMarginController whenever the panel's
paper size or orientation changes. Surfaced by code review of the ppi.1 print
work; out of scope for that PR (which clamps correctly for the default paper).
close_reason: Added MPPrintMarginSynchronizer: re-clamps the operation's margins when the imageable area changes mid-print-session, observing paperSize/orientation/printer (PR #15). Testing reframed the bead's premise: on current macOS paper size & orientation are changed via Page Setup, NOT the print panel, and that path is already handled by -printInfo (verified — landscape correctly down-caps top/bottom). So the synchronizer's live in-panel trigger is the destination-printer change; paperSize/orientation observation is kept as defensive cover for systems/drivers that expose them. Strictly additive (left/right always re-clamp; top/bottom re-clamp unless WebKit restores cached values, in which case it matches pre-PR behavior — never a regression). Comments/scope reframed accordingly. Kept per owner's call; revisit if printer-change handling ever causes trouble.

title: Vendor hoedown (and LibYAML) as SPM clang targets
description: Phase 2 de-pod template for macdown-5xp. Vendor the hoedown C source into an SPM clang target we own, removing the patched MacDownApp/cocoapods-specs hoedown pod. This is the template for moving a C/Obj-C dependency into a package, and it unblocks moving MPRenderer into an SPM target later. LibYAML (also C) can follow the same pattern. Keep the local Extension/hoedown_html_patch.c patch.
close_reason: hoedown 3.0.7 vendored as local SPM package (Packages/Hoedown), hoedown pod removed; app builds + 114 tests green; de-pod template established for LibYAML and the rest of 8tk.7.

title: Stand up local SPM package(s) and link from the app target
description: Phase 1 of macdown-5xp. Create a local in-repo SPM package (e.g. MacDownKit, or several targets: Utilities / MarkdownRendering / EditorCore / UI) and add it to MacDown.xcodeproj as a local package dependency the app target links. Start small to establish the thin-shell-links-packages topology. A module can only MOVE into a package once its CocoaPods deps are on SPM (see de-pod work in 8tk.7).
close_reason: Phase 1: MacDownKit local SPM package stood up and linked from app target; two leaf categories moved in; build + 114 tests green.

title: Enable Swift/Obj-C interop (bridging header + incremental nullability)
description: Phase 0 of macdown-5xp. Add the first .swift file so Xcode auto-creates the bridging header, then add NS_ASSUME_NONNULL + lightweight-generics annotations ONLY to the Obj-C headers that Swift file imports (incremental). ~30 min; enables per-class Obj-C/Swift choice in the app target. Do it WITH the first real Swift code (likely ppi.1 PDF export), not speculatively. The Modern Obj-C Converter (8tk.9) is NOT a prerequisite.
close_reason: Swift enabled (SWIFT_VERSION 5.0 + bridging header); exercised both directions by the print-margin code.

title: Fix -Wstrict-prototypes in macdown-cmd/main.m
description: CI (macdown-8tk.6) surfaced two function-declaration-without-prototype warnings in macdown-cmd/main.m (lines ~20 and ~53). The macdown-cmd CLI target was not in scope of macdown-8tk.2 (app + pmh sources only). Mechanical (void) fix, same as 8tk.2.
close_reason: Added (void) to the two empty parameter lists in macdown-cmd/main.m (MPRunningMacDownInstance, MPPipedData). Build clean, no -Wstrict-prototypes warnings remain.

title: Scroll-sync: editor per-line regex diverges from hoedown block parser (code fences, no-space ATX, etc.)
description:
Follow-up from the macdown-4y8 review. The editor's reference-node scan (-updateEditorHeaderLocations in MPDocument.m) classifies lines with per-line regexes, while the preview selects nodes from hoedown's fully-parsed DOM (h1..h6, p>img:only-child). The two must stay index-symmetric for scroll-sync. macdown-4y8 fixed the three documented cases (setext H1, setext anchor Y, img selector); this tracks the remaining PRE-EXISTING divergences a hoedown-source review found, all from the regex model lacking block context:

1. Code fences: header-like or rule-like lines inside a triple-backtick fenced code block are literal text in the preview (no anchor) but the editor still matches them, producing phantom editor anchors. Highest real-world impact (docs routinely contain hash comments and ASCII rules inside code).
2. No-space ATX: with HOEDOWN_EXT_SPACE_HEADERS off (MacDown default), hoedown renders a hash with no following space as a heading, but the editor pattern requires a space, giving a preview-only anchor.
3. Trailing/leading whitespace on a setext underline: hoedown's is_headerline tolerates trailing spaces; the editor pattern does not, giving a preview-only anchor.
4. Images in blockquotes or list items, or leading-space-indented images: the editor regex is start-anchored and misses them; the blockquote case still yields a preview p>img:only-child, giving a preview-only anchor.
5. ATX header immediately followed by a setext rule: the editor emits BOTH an ATX anchor and a setext anchor for the same heading, a double-count versus one preview heading element.
6. Multi-line paragraph closed by a setext underline: the editor anchors the line immediately above the underline unconditionally; hoedown only makes the LAST paragraph line the heading.

Root cause / altitude: reference-node identity is defined twice in two languages (CSS selector vs Obj-C regex plus state), with symmetry enforced only by comments and tests. Deeper fix options: (a) make the editor scan block-aware (track code-fence/list/blockquote state, no-space ATX, underline whitespace), or (b) single source of truth -- derive editor reference-node positions from the renderer's block structure rather than re-deriving via regex. Acceptance: a fixture mixing all six cases yields equal-length, same-order editor and preview anchor arrays. Needs runtime scroll verification.
notes:
Update 2 (4y8 PR #12, round 3): the #3 image-selector tightening (p>img:only-child) was attempted and then REVERTED to the original img:only-child. It was a lateral trade: it fixed linked images but regressed lazy list-item images, because CSS :only-child ignores text nodes, so img:only-child already matched
  • item
    • . So ALL image reference-node alignment now lives in this bead: linked images, inline image with a text sibling, images in lists/blockquotes/indented, and lazy list continuations. Candidate approach (from Gemini): JS post-filter the querySelectorAll results to drop images whose parent has non-whitespace textContent, combined with block-aware editor scanning. Also in scope: setext-underline whitespace tolerance (hoedown allows up to 3 leading spaces and trailing whitespace; the editor regex does not).

title: Broaden file:// rewriting: unencoded paths + more URL-bearing attributes
description: MPHTMLByRewritingFileURLsToAssetScheme (added in macdown-8tk.5.8) only rewrites src=/href= attributes and parses each captured value with [NSURL URLWithString:]. Two narrowings vs the legacy WebView remain: (1) a hand-written file:// URL containing a literal space or non-ASCII char (e.g. file:///My Photos/x.png typed directly, NOT via toggleImage: which percent-encodes via -absoluteString) makes URLWithString: return nil, so it is skipped and stays file:// — WKWebView then blocks it. The old WebView's HTML parser percent-encoded such URLs and loaded them. (2) Other URL-bearing forms are not rewritten: srcset, poster, <source src>, <video>/<audio> src, <object data>, and CSS url(file://...) in inline style or <style>. A deeper fix would resolve local URLs at the scheme-handler/navigation boundary (where the request is already a parsed NSURL) rather than via a pre-parse string regex, subsuming relative/bare-absolute/file:// under one path. Surfaced by code-review on the macdown-8tk.5.8 branch.
notes: Also (PR #11 Codex review): because the rewrite runs over the full serialized HTML string, file: URLs inside raw passthrough HTML — e.g. a string literal in a hand-written <script>, or text in <pre> — are matched and rewritten too, silently altering user-authored script/text. Same root cause as the src/href-only narrowness: operate on parsed request URLs at the scheme-handler/navigation boundary instead of a pre-parse string regex.

title: Refresh non-English Settings window-title translations to Apple's current terms
description:
The Settings window title now shows "Settings" in English (macdown-e5e). Its NSLocalizedString key was deliberately kept as "Preferences" so the 17 existing translations keep resolving, and English is overridden to "Settings" in en.lproj. However, several non-English translations still use the pre-Ventura term (fr "Préférences", ja "環境設定", zh-Hans "偏好设置", ...), which now MISMATCHES the app-menu item: on macOS 13+ AppKit auto-renames that menu item to Apple's current localized term (verified at runtime: fr "Réglages…", ja "設定…", zh-Hans "设置…", ru "Настройки..."). So in those locales the window says one thing and the menu another.

Fix: update the window-title term in each affected language to Apple's current standard wording, sourced from Apple's macOS Localization Glossary (developer.apple.com/localization/resources; searchable mirrors applelocalization.com / xcstrings.com). Apply through Transifex (do NOT hand-edit the non-English .strings directly — they round-trip per CLAUDE.md). Some locales already match (de "Einstellungen") and need no change.

NOTE: a runtime approach (reading the menu item's title and stripping the ellipsis) was investigated and REJECTED — the menu title format varies by locale (U+2026 vs literal "...", a space before the ellipsis in de, and ar not renamed at all), so string-stripping is too fragile. The glossary-via-Transifex path is the standard solution.

Acceptance: in each shipped locale the Settings window title matches Apple's current macOS terminology and the app-menu "Settings…" item (modulo the trailing ellipsis).

title: copyFiles never refreshes bundled Styles/Themes in the user data dir
description:
MPMainController -copyFiles copies bundled Styles/ and Themes/ into the user data dir (~/Library/Application Support/<CFBundleName>/) only when a file is MISSING — it never overwrites an existing copy (intentional, to preserve user edits). Combined with MPStylePathForName reading from the data dir (not the bundle), this means updates to bundled styles/themes never reach users who already have a copy.

Surfaced by macdown-4d4: the old broken generator wrote a 0-byte GitHub-2020.css into many users' data dirs; even after 4d4 ships a correct bundled CSS, copyFiles will keep the stale empty copy, so the GitHub-2020 style still renders unstyled for anyone who ran a broken build (revival developers especially). Fresh installs are fine (everything copies). Manually deleting the data-dir copy works around it.

Possible fixes to weigh:
- Overwrite a data-dir style/theme when the bundled file is non-empty and the data-dir copy is 0 bytes (targeted self-heal for exactly this corruption; safe — a 0-byte style is never user-authored).
- Version/checksum the bundled assets and refresh data-dir copies that match a previously-shipped bundled version (preserves genuine user edits, updates untouched defaults). More involved.
- Leave as-is and document that users must delete a stale style to get an update.
Pre-existing behavior, not introduced by the revival.
close_reason: copyFiles now treats a 0-byte data-dir style/theme like a missing one and replaces it with the bundled default (won't clobber non-empty user edits)

title: Terminal prefs: dead 'location'/'supportText' outlets (missing setter/ivar)
description:
Launch console (Xcode, 2026-06-14) logs while loading the Terminal preferences pane:
  'Failed to connect (location) outlet from (MPTerminalPreferencesViewController) to (NSTextField): missing setter or instance variable'
  'Failed to connect (supportText) outlet from (MPTerminalPreferencesViewController) to (NSTextField): missing setter or instance variable'

The Terminal preferences xib wires two NSTextField outlets ('location', 'supportText') to MPTerminalPreferencesViewController, but the class no longer declares those properties/ivars, so the connections silently fail at nib load. Likely leftover from a past refactor. Either restore the properties (if the fields should be bound) or remove the stale connections from the xib. Low impact (the pane still loads), but it's nib-load noise and means those fields aren't wired to code. Discovered while investigating macdown-8tk.3.
close_reason: Fixed in batch PR (transparent-divider normalization + stale Terminal-prefs outlet removal)

title: Scroll-sync: editor/preview reference-node anchor asymmetry
description:
CodeRabbit flagged on PR #7 (scroll-sync, macdown-8tk.5.4). The editor and preview anchor-extraction must stay index-symmetric for the interpolation to map correctly; today they can diverge, which would drift the whole mapping after the first mismatched node. This is PRE-EXISTING behavior faithfully ported from the legacy WebView implementation, not introduced by the WKWebView migration, so it's tracked separately rather than fixed in the migration PR.

Known asymmetries:
1. Setext H1: the editor scan's dashRegex is '^([-]+)$' — it matches setext-H2 underlines ('---') but NOT setext-H1 underlines ('==='). A setext H1 therefore produces an <h1> anchor in the preview (selected by 'h1') with no corresponding editor anchor -> count mismatch -> drift. Fix: match '^([-=]+)$'.
2. Setext anchor Y: the editor anchors a setext heading from the UNDERLINE line; the preview anchors from the rendered heading top. Same count, but slightly different Y. Minor; consider anchoring the editor from the heading text line (the line before the underline).
3. img selector: editor matches a line that is exactly an image ('^!\[...\]\(...\)$'); preview matches 'img:only-child', which can select preview-only inline cases. Consider tightening to 'p > img:only-child' or aligning both rules.

Acceptance: build a test doc mixing ATX headers, setext H1/H2, and standalone images; confirm editor and preview produce equal-length anchor arrays and scroll stays aligned through the whole doc. Needs runtime verification (image/setext fixture) before changing the working ATX-header path.

CodeRabbit suggested-diff is in the PR #7 thread for reference.
notes: Code fix done on branch revival/scroll-sync-anchor-symmetry (build clean, 57/57 tests). Three asymmetries addressed, ATX path untouched: (#1) editor dashRegex ^([-]+)$ -> ^([-=]+)$ so setext H1 '===' anchors like the preview's <h1>; (#2) setext anchors now computed from the heading TEXT line (line above the underline), matching the preview's <h1>/<h2> top; (#3) preview selector img:only-child -> p>img:only-child so linked/list/inline images aren't preview-only anchors. Added 4 prompt-free render tests to MPRendererTests.m pinning the preview-side HTML shapes. DEFERRED (needs GUI, user AFK): runtime fixture scroll-alignment check + editor-side anchor-extraction characterization -> see follow-up bead. Known remaining edge: a blockquoted standalone image '> ![](x)' still yields a preview-only p>img anchor (rare; was worse before).
close_reason: Fixed the 3 documented reference-node asymmetries (setext H1 match, setext anchor from heading-text line, preview p>img:only-child); high-effort review applied (homogeneous-run regex ^(=+|-+)$, single-source isDashLine, exact-string render tests). Build clean, 57/57 tests. Runtime mixed-fixture scroll verification to be done by Stuart before merging the PR. Deeper pre-existing editor-regex vs hoedown-block-parser divergences tracked in macdown-y9j.

title: Fix GitHub-2020 style generator (node-sass deprecated; emits empty CSS)
description:
The "Transpile Styles" Xcode build phase runs 'make -C Tools/GitHub-style-generator/', which generates MacDown/Resources/Styles/GitHub-2020.css from index.sass (@import primer-markdown) via node-sass.

Two problems on a modern toolchain:
1. node-sass is long deprecated (superseded by dart-sass/'sass'). node_modules is not installed in the tool dir, so the node-sass binary is missing.
2. The Makefile recipe is 'node-sass ... | sed ... > $@'. The '> $@' redirect creates/truncates the target to 0 bytes BEFORE the pipeline runs, so when node-sass fails to launch the build silently bundles an EMPTY GitHub-2020.css instead of failing. (This empty file kept reappearing as untracked working-tree noise; it is now gitignored and deleted via 'make clean'.)

Impact: every local build that runs this phase bundles an empty 'GitHub-2020' preview stylesheet -> that theme renders unstyled.

Fix options to weigh:
- Migrate node-sass -> dart-sass ('sass' npm pkg or Homebrew 'sass'); update Makefile + package.json/package-lock.json.
- Make the recipe atomic + fail-loud: write to a temp file and mv on success, or use 'set -o pipefail' / check node-sass exit, so a missing toolchain fails the build instead of emitting empty CSS.
- Decide whether GitHub-2020.css should stay build-generated (keep gitignored) or be pre-generated and committed like GitHub.css/GitHub2.css.

Context: discovered during macdown-8tk.5.4 (WKWebView scroll-sync); the empty file had been following several beads around.
close_reason: Replaced node-sass+primer-markdown@4 generator with a curl/github-markdown-css script + committed CSS; dropped the Transpile Styles build phase. Plus copyFiles empty-file self-heal (112) and Browser-default label.

title: Transparent preview background makes the editor-hidden divider invisible
description: Pre-existing (not introduced by the WKWebView migration; the legacy WebView path had identical behavior). When the preview body has no explicit background, getComputedStyle returns rgba(0,0,0,0); colorWithHTMLName parses it to a valid alpha-0 NSColor, so -redrawDivider sets the split divider to a fully transparent color when the editor is hidden (rather than nil -> the superclass default). Consider treating an (near-)transparent parsed color as nil so the default divider shows. Surfaced during PR review of macdown-8tk.5.3.
close_reason: Fixed in batch PR (transparent-divider normalization + stale Terminal-prefs outlet removal)

title: Serve preview assets off the main thread in MPAssetSchemeHandler
description: MPAssetSchemeHandler reads files synchronously with dataWithContentsOfFile: inside startURLSchemeTask:, which WebKit invokes on the main thread. For large assets (mermaid.min.js ~1.1MB, MathJax) this briefly blocks the UI. Move the read+reply to a background queue (WKURLSchemeTask callbacks need only be serialized per task, not on the main thread), keeping the liveTasks guard. Low priority: local-disk reads are fast.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #4 review round 1: MPAssetSchemeHandler now reads off the main thread (global queue) and replies on the main queue, guarded by liveTasks.

title: Fix editor image drag-and-drop (registration, non-file drags, MIME)
description:
Latent issues in MPEditorView drag-and-drop, surfaced during code review of the macdown-8tk.1 deprecation-cleanup PR and deferred to keep that PR behaviour-neutral:
1. -awakeFromNib registers NSPasteboardNameDrag (a pasteboard NAME) via registerForDraggedTypes:, which expects drag TYPES. Should register NSPasteboardTypeFileURL to match the file-URL reads in -performDragOperation:. (Pre-existing: the old code registered NSDragPboard, equally wrong.)
2. -performDragOperation: only handles file URLs, but -draggingEntered: advertises acceptance for any public.jpeg. Dragging an image from a browser or Photos (non-file URL / raw image data) shows an accept cursor then silently no-ops.
3. A dropped image is always embedded as data:image/jpeg regardless of real type, so a PNG/GIF gets a wrong MIME in the data URI. Gate/convert by actual UTType.
Proper fix needs a focused drag-and-drop pass with manual testing of file/browser/Photos image drags.

title: Modern Objective-C Converter (optional syntax cleanup; not the Swift gate)
description:
Run Xcode's Edit > Convert > To Modern Objective-C Syntax across the MP* sources for cosmetic modernization: instancetype, NS_ENUM/NS_OPTIONS, container subscripting, @-literals, property dot-syntax.

CLARIFICATION (corrects the original premise): the converter is NOT what enables Swift interop, and this bead is NOT a prerequisite for writing Swift. Swift in this project needs only (1) a bridging header, which Xcode auto-creates the moment the first .swift file is added (~5 min), and (2) nullability + lightweight-generics annotations (NS_ASSUME_NONNULL_BEGIN, NSArray<T *> *) on the SPECIFIC Obj-C headers a Swift file imports, added incrementally/lazily when that Swift file is written. The Modern Obj-C Converter does NOT add nullability/generics (that part is manual), so it buys little for interop.

Therefore: deprioritized, optional, opportunistic. Do it only if/when the cosmetic cleanup is wanted; never re-run it on files being actively rewritten (e.g. mid-WKWebView-migration MPDocument.m) since it just creates churn/conflicts. Not on the critical path.
notes: Deferred 2026-06-14 (decision): hold the Modern Obj-C Converter run until the WKWebView migration (8tk.5, incl. 8tk.5.5 finalize+delete-legacy) lands. Rationale: the converter's prime target is MPDocument.m, which the migration is actively rewriting; running it now creates churn/conflicts (the bead's own warning) and would convert code about to be deleted. When unblocked, run across all MP* sources in one pass on branch revival/modern-objc-syntax. Mechanics: converter is interactive Xcode-only (Edit > Convert > To Modern Objective-C Syntax) -> Stuart drives it in Xcode; Claude reviews the diff vs conventions (80-col, Allman braces), builds, runs tests, commits.

title: Improve PDF export & printing: margins + no mid-line page breaks (keep WebKit engine)
description:
Improve PDF export and printing within the existing engine. Decided
(2026-06-14): do NOT change rendering engines. PDF export and printing both
already run through WKWebView's native print operation
(-printOperationWithPrintInfo:, MPDocument.m), which is the only WYSIWYG path
(same WebKit, HTML, theme CSS, fonts as the live preview) and paginates
between line boxes. weasyprint / headless Chromium / pandoc-LaTeX were
considered and rejected here: each is a second renderer that diverges from the
preview and adds heavy runtime/toolchain cost. Revisit only on a concrete
showstopper that names the problem it solves.

Scope of this bead (three concrete defects in the current path):

1. Margin preferences. Today MPDocument -printInfo hard-codes top/bottom = 50pt
   and left/right = 0pt with no user control. Add preferred-margin preferences
   (MPPreferences) surfaced in the print/export UI.

2. Printable-area validation. Zero/with small margins, content is laid out into
   the printer's non-imageable border and gets clipped ("prints beyond the
   printable area"). At print/export time, clamp requested margins to the
   device's imageable bounds for the chosen paper size and warn when the
   request was too small.

3. No mid-line page breaks. Ensure a page break never splits a line of text.
   WebKit already breaks between line boxes by default; back that up with a
   MacDown-owned print stylesheet (injected for print/export, independent of
   the chosen preview theme) carrying orphans/widows so paragraphs don't strand
   single lines. (Today the @media print rules live inconsistently inside each
   theme: GitHub/Clearness/GitHub2 have page-break-inside:avoid, Solarized has
   none.)

Initial goal: do not break TEXT lines. Clean page breaks for images and tables
are explicitly OUT of scope here (harder; track separately if wanted).

First Swift component (pulls in 5xp.1): the margin-resolution/validation logic
is pure geometry and is the natural first self-contained Swift unit, wrapped by
a Swift print/export controller called from the Obj-C MPDocument.
design:
Engine: KEEP WKWebView -printOperationWithPrintInfo: (already in place). Not an
engine-selection task.

Test-first plan, split by testability:
- Tier 1 (pure logic, true TDD): margin resolution/validation. A pure function
  (paperSize, imageableRect, requestedMargins) -> (effectiveMargins, wasClamped).
  Encodes defect #2 directly; no printer needed. Failing test first: requested
  0pt side margin on a device with an N-pt hardware margin -> effective >= N and
  wasClamped == true. Today's code (left/right = 0, no clamp) fails it.
- Tier 2 (characterization, like MPRendererTests): the injected print stylesheet
  is a pure string producer -> assert it carries orphans/widows + break rules.
- Tier 3 (integration, likely local-only): render a tall doc through the export
  path to PDF data, open with PDFKit, assert multi-page and no text lost across
  boundaries. Headless WKWebView printing can be flaky in CI -> may gate/skip on
  CI. Cannot finely assert "no line visually clipped"; the guarantee comes from
  WebKit's between-line-box pagination + orphans/widows, not from this test.

Before asserting a line-split FIX, confirm empirically whether the current
engine even splits text lines mid-glyph (generate one sample PDF). If it never
does, the line-split test is a regression guard, and effort concentrates on the
margin work (the real, reproducible bug).
close_reason: Margins clamped to imageable area + preferences + Print pane; MacDown print stylesheet for page breaks; interactive-print WKPrintingView fix. Follow-ups: macdown-evb, macdown-wh6.

title: New features (post-revival)
description: Home for feature work layered on top of the revival. MacDown is being revived as-is AND extended with new capabilities.

title: Establish fork release identity
description: Bundle id and update feed still point at the original author (com.uranusjr.macdown, macdown.uranusjr.com). A real fork release needs its own bundle id, Sparkle appcast feed, and signing identity (current dsa_pub.pem is the original's).
design: Decision (2026-06-14): adopt Sparkle 2 (2.9.3) via SPM, not a 1.x bump. Rationale: the SPM binary xcframework is universal so it fixes the arm64 load failure; SPM matches the de-pod direction (macdown-5xp); and Sparkle 2 EdDSA forces the clean break from the original DSA keys/feed this issue already requires. Cost: API migration SUUpdater -> SPUStandardUpdaterController (see 8tk.4) and generating a fresh EdDSA keypair + own SUFeedURL.
notes: Sparkle/auto-update work folded in here from macdown-8tk.4 (2026-06-14): the pinned Sparkle 1.18.1 is x86_64-only and can't load on arm64, and the framework is dead-stripped (SUUpdater is nib-only). Fixing Check-for-Updates needs a universal Sparkle bump (1.27.3 drop-in, or 2.9.3 with API migration) + a force-link reference — but updates won't deliver until this issue provides the fork's own SUFeedURL + signing keys. See macdown-8tk.4 notes for the full diagnosis and fix recipe. Do them together.

title: De-pod: move CocoaPods dependencies to SPM (audit each)
description: Reframed 2026-06-14 from bump-pinned-pods to the de-pod direction (CocoaPods is being retired; see macdown-5xp). Audit each pinned pod and choose: move to SPM, vendor the source as an SPM target we own, or replace. Per-pod plan: Sparkle -> SPM binary xcframework, universal, fixes arm64 (see 8tk.4 / 8tk.8); hoedown + LibYAML -> vendor as SPM clang targets (macdown-5xp.3); handlebars-objc -> vendor or replace; PAPreferences + MASPreferences -> vendor or replace (MASPreferences is a candidate to replace with a SwiftUI Settings scene). PAPreferences was the original reason for the macOS 10.8 floor, now moot.
notes: CI (8tk.6) warning: the pinned Sparkle pod ships an x86_64-only vendored binary ('none of which match arm64'), so auto-update is non-functional on Apple Silicon. A newer Sparkle (universal/arm64) is needed — fold into this pod bump, and it also affects the release identity work (8tk.8).

title: Set up CI on GitHub Actions
description: Replace defunct Travis (.travis.yml, xcode10.1). Build + test the MacDown scheme on a modern macOS runner. Worth doing early so later PRs get CI. Mind the multi-step setup: git submodules, modern Bundler, bundle exec pod install, make -C Dependency/peg-markdown-highlight.
notes: Deprioritized P2->P3 (2026-06-13, after discussion). For a single-maintainer project the 'build+test before every PR' discipline covers regression-catching as well as a CI gate, and faster. CI's distinctive value is NOT regressions but: (a) clean-room build from a fresh clone — verifies the fragile submodule/bundle/pod/make setup works and that nothing needed is uncommitted; (b) an Xcode/macOS matrix to confirm the macOS 12 deployment floor actually runs (we only ever build on 27). Don't gate PRs on it. Revisit as a NON-BLOCKING job before a release, or when an external contributor opens a PR.
close_reason: GitHub Actions CI added (.github/workflows/ci.yml): builds + tests on latest-stable Xcode / macos-latest, on push to master, PRs, and manual dispatch. Verified green on PR #13's own run (all steps pass). Handles submodules, CocoaPods-via-Bundler, the pmh_parser.c make step, and headless ad-hoc-signed tests. Replaces the defunct Travis config.
